title: calathiana verna subsp. tergestina
intro: Calathiana verna subsp. tergestina, also known as the Alpine Buttercup, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Alps region of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The Alpine Buttercup can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, from colds and fever to skin conditions and digestive issues.
